rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid scopes: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees over visible ports, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or references not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: Fix diagnostics by resolving root causes of invalid references or type errors before adding new clauses.
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
When a guarantee references implementation-level features, move it to the appropriate implementation annex.
Avoid generating unsafe clauses that compare opaque data types with numeric values.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex scope.
anti_patterns: Do not add new clauses to fix unresolved references; instead, trace and resolve the root cause.
Do not generate Boolean-to-integer conversions for opaque data ports.
irrelevant_topics: component type vs implementation scope distinctions in model analysis
